figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of query generation approaches: naive LLM generation produces platform-specific terminology and ungrounded features; contrastive-only generation grounds queries in listings but produces verbose output; our seed-guided approach produces realistic, terse queries matching real user behavior

Figure 1: Contrastive query generation pipeline. Left: input data sources include search sessions (with booking logs) and listing features (descriptions, amenities, reviews). Center: sessions with bookings are filtered to extract booked and non-booked listing pairs, which are enriched with catalog features. Right: LLM processing combines listing pairs with seed queries and prompting strategies to generate synthetic queries with topicality labels by construction.

Table 2: Comparison of query characteristics across datasets. Baseline (contrastive without seed) produces verbose queries; our approach with seed guidance achieves better alignment with real user behavior.

Figure 3: Query length distribution comparison across datasets. Real users show right-skewed distribution with peak at 2 words; seed data shows uniform spread; our approach concentrates at 5–6 words; baseline is extremely verbose with 57% at 15+ words.
